quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 6 Quiz 4
quiz.subtitle: Diagonal Properties and Perpendicular/Congruent Characteristics
quiz.subject: Geometry
quiz.topic: Geometry: 6.4 Properties of Rhombuses and Rectangles

questions.question: A rhombus is defined as a parallelogram with:
options: Exactly one pair of parallel sides
questions.explanation: A rhombus is a special type of parallelogram in which all four sides are congruent. It inherits all parallelogram properties.

questions.question: A rectangle is defined as a parallelogram with:
options: Exactly one pair of parallel sides
questions.explanation: A rectangle is a special type of parallelogram in which all four angles are congruent (each $90^\circ$). It inherits all parallelogram properties.

questions.question: Which property is true for the diagonals of a rhombus?
options: The diagonals do not bisect each other
questions.explanation: The diagonals of a rhombus are perpendicular to each other. They also bisect each other (inherited from parallelograms) and bisect the angles of the rhombus.

questions.question: Which property is true for the diagonals of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: The diagonals of a rectangle are congruent. They also bisect each other (inherited from parallelograms). Unlike a rhombus, they are not necessarily perpendicular.

questions.question: Which statement is true for a rhombus but NOT for a general parallelogram?
questions.explanation: Perpendicular diagonals are a special property of rhombuses (and squares). General parallelograms do not have perpendicular diagonals. The other properties hold for all parallelograms.

questions.question: Which statement is true for a rectangle but NOT for a general parallelogram?
questions.explanation: Congruent diagonals are a special property of rectangles (and squares). General parallelograms do not have congruent diagonals. The other properties hold for all parallelograms.

questions.question: In rhombus $ABCD$, $m\angle A = 50^\circ$. What is $m\angle B$?
questions.explanation: A rhombus is a parallelogram, so consecutive angles are supplementary. $m\angle B = 180^\circ - 50^\circ = 130^\circ$.

questions.question: In rhombus $WXYZ$, the diagonals intersect at point $P$. If $WP = 5$ and $XP = 12$, what is the length of side $WX$?
questions.explanation: The diagonals of a rhombus are perpendicular and bisect each other. The diagonals form four right triangles with legs $5$ and $12$. By the Pythagorean Theorem: $WX = \sqrt{5^2 + 12^2} = \sqrt{25 + 144} = \sqrt{169} = 13$.

questions.question: In rectangle $ABCD$, diagonal $AC = 10$. What is $BD$?
questions.explanation: The diagonals of a rectangle are congruent. Since $AC = 10$, diagonal $BD$ also measures $10$.

questions.question: A quadrilateral is both a rhombus and a rectangle. What must it be?
questions.explanation: A square has all four sides congruent (rhombus property) and all four angles congruent (rectangle property). Any quadrilateral that is both a rhombus and a rectangle is a square.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the properties of rhombuses and rectangles, including their diagonal characteristics and how they relate to parallelograms.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the main properties but review the difference: rhombus diagonals are perpendicular, rectangle diagonals are congruent.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: rhombus = parallelogram with four congruent sides and perpendicular diagonals; rectangle = parallelogram with four right angles and congruent diagonals.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! A rhombus has perpendicular diagonals that bisect the angles. A rectangle has congruent diagonals. Both inherit all parallelogram properties. A square has both sets of properties.
